    "title": "NPM Distribution System Architecture Designed",
    "subtitle": "Complete specification for `npx founder-os@latest --init` command enabling one-command installation of founderOS into any project.",
    "facts": "[\"Spec [14] defines npm package `founder-os` with CLI entry point installing to `.founderOS/` (infrastructure) and `.claude/` (commands/skills/agents)\",\"Installation is idempotent using manifest.json with SHA-256 checksums to detect user modifications and skip overwriting customized files\",\"CLI supports --init (install/update), --version, --status, --uninstall, and --force flags for complete lifecycle management\",\"CLAUDE.md generation uses `<!-- founder-os:start -->` markers to separate managed content from user content, enabling safe updates\",\"settings.json uses deep-merge strategy preserving user configurations while adding founderOS MCP server configs\",\"Release pipeline defined: dev repo → template assembly → version bump → npm publish from founder-os-dist repo\",\"Technical constraints: Node.js >= 18, no runtime dependencies, ~200 line CLI, synchronous file operations, sub-5-second install\",\"Update flow compares checksums per-file: unchanged files overwritten, modified files skipped with warnings, deleted files marked as user-removed\"]",
    "narrative": "A comprehensive architectural specification for distributing founderOS via npm was designed. The system enables users to run `npx founder-os@latest --init` to install all 37 command namespaces, 12 agent teams, and infrastructure into their project. The design splits content into `.founderOS/` (engine: dispatcher, memory, intelligence, scripts) and `.claude/` (interface: commands, skills, agents, hooks, settings). A manifest.json file tracks all managed files with SHA-256 checksums, enabling intelligent updates that preserve user customizations. The CLI detects modifications by comparing checksums and skips overwriting user-edited files while updating unchanged ones. CLAUDE.md uses HTML comment markers to separate founderOS-managed documentation from user content. The settings.json merge strategy deep-merges configurations to add MCP servers without clobbering user settings. The release pipeline assembles templates from the dev repo, bumps versions, and publishes to npm. Edge cases are handled including partial installs, permission errors, name collisions (user files take precedence), and uninstalls with modifications (requires --force). Success criteria include sub-5-second install, preservation of all user modifications on update, and clean uninstall capability.",

    "facts": "[\"Design specification created at founderOS/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-scout-namespace-design.md documenting P34 Scout namespace\",\"Scout namespace automates discovery of Claude Code skills, MCP servers, GitHub repos, and CLI packages with security review\",\"Seven commands designed: scout:find (discovery), scout:install (sandboxed install), scout:catalog (browse), scout:review (security re-scan), scout:promote (move to native namespace), scout:remove (uninstall), scout:sources (configure)\",\"Skills-first search priority: Claude Code skills (priority 1) > MCP servers (priority 2) > GitHub repos (priority 3) > npm/PyPI CLI tools (priority 4)\",\"Security sandbox implements 6-point scan checking for prompt injection, secret exfiltration, overly broad tools, data leakage, supply chain risk, and permission escalation\",\"Three-layer caching strategy: catalog.json (zero-token lookup), Memory Engine (semantic recall ~200-500 tokens), web search (budget-controlled 1,000-2,000 tokens per call)\",\"Token cost estimates: cache hit ~500 tokens, fresh search ~8,000-12,000 tokens, full install with security review ~15,000-20,000 tokens\",\"Four skills defined: scout-research (search strategy), scout-security (security checklist), scout-integration (wrapper templates), scout-common (catalog schema and sandbox management)\",\"Generated wrapper commands immediately usable in P27 workflow YAML pipelines before promotion to native namespaces\",\"Graceful degradation: operates in catalog-only mode when WebSearch unavailable, optional --deep mode activates multi-agent team\"]",
    "narrative": "Comprehensive design specification created for the Scout namespace (P34), a new external tool and skill discovery system for founderOS. The design solves the problem of manual tool research and integration by automating discovery, security review, and wrapper generation for external solutions. The system follows a skills-first priority cascade per Anthropic guidance, checking Claude Code skills first (zero-install, native format), then MCP servers, GitHub repos, and finally CLI packages. Every discovered tool passes through a security sandbox with a 6-point scan covering prompt injection, secret exfiltration, overly broad permissions, data leakage, supply chain risks, and permission escalation. Security verdicts (green/yellow/red) are non-blocking but prominently displayed and logged. The design emphasizes token efficiency with a three-layer caching strategy: zero-token catalog lookups, semantic Memory Engine recall, and budget-controlled web searches. Discovered tools are automatically wrapped as founderOS commands in the scout namespace and immediately usable in workflow YAML before optional promotion to native namespaces. The specification includes detailed command schemas, security review processes, wrapper generation templates, agent team configuration for deep mode, token cost estimates, and an end-to-end example workflow. This design enables users to solve new problems by discovering and safely integrating external tools without leaving the founderOS ecosystem.",

    "title": "Adaptive Intelligence Engine Architecture Specification",
    "subtitle": "Designed hooks-first intelligence layer enabling plugins to learn preferences, self-heal from errors, and gradually increase autonomy through confidence-gated pattern application",
    "facts": "[\"Architecture separates infrastructure layer (_infrastructure/intelligence/) from user-facing Intelligence Hub plugin (founder-os-intelligence-hub/)\",\"Three core modules implemented: Hooks (event capture), Learning (observe-retrieve-judge-distill-consolidate-apply cycle), Self-Healing (error classification with retry/degradation)\",\"Learning system operates in three tiers: Tier 1 taste-learning (output preferences), Tier 2 workflow-optimization (command chain detection), Tier 3 confidence-gating (graduated autonomy)\",\"Hook system captures five event types: pre_command, post_command, mcp_call, decision_point, error stored in SQLite with 30-day retention\",\"Pattern confidence calculated as confirmations / (confirmations + rejections * 2), auto-applied at confidence >= 0.5 with 3+ observations\",\"Error classification uses four categories: Transient (retry with backoff), Recoverable (fix + retry), Degradable (fallback + continue), Fatal (stop + notify)\",\"Six slash commands provide user control: /intel:status, /intel:patterns, /intel:approve, /intel:reset, /intel:healing, /intel:config\",\"Patterns sync bidirectionally with Notion database containing confidence scores, observation counts, and injectable instructions\",\"Plugin integration uses convention-based observation blocks rather than requiring framework dependencies\",\"Architecture adapted from ruflo/claude-flow v3.5 SONA system, modified for single-execution plugin commands instead of multi-agent swarms\"]",
    "narrative": "Created comprehensive design specification for an Adaptive Intelligence Engine that adds learning and self-healing capabilities to the Founder OS plugin ecosystem. The architecture uses a hooks-first approach with a separate behavioral layer built on top of the existing Memory Engine. The infrastructure layer provides four modules (hooks, learning, self-healing, routing) backed by a dedicated SQLite database, while a user-facing Intelligence Hub plugin exposes control through six slash commands. The hooks system captures structured events at command-level, MCP-level, and decision-point-level, storing them with session context for pattern analysis. The learning system implements a retrieve-judge-distill-consolidate-route cycle that observes plugin executions, detects repeated patterns, calculates confidence scores based on user confirmations vs rejections, and injects learned preferences as instructions into future runs. Learning operates in three graduated tiers: taste-learning for output preferences (ships first), workflow-optimization for command chain detection, and confidence-gating for autonomous decision-making. The self-healing module classifies errors into four categories with distinct handling strategies—transient errors retry with exponential backoff, recoverable errors apply known fixes, degradable errors execute fallback paths, and fatal errors stop with clear user guidance. A fallback registry ships with seed data for all 30 plugins defining graceful degradation paths. The system tracks healing effectiveness and reclassifies error categories over time. Patterns sync bidirectionally with a Notion database, allowing users to review and edit learned behaviors outside the CLI. The specification includes complete database schemas, confidence mechanics, plugin integration conventions, and a seven-phase ship order prioritizing immediate observability value through hooks before rolling out adaptive features. Architecture inspired by ruflo/claude-flow v3.5 but adapted for single-execution plugin commands rather than long-running multi-agent swarms.",

    "narrative": "Deployed a complete Founder OS HQ Notion workspace to the user's Notion account with [FOS] prefix for namespace identification. The deployment created 1 parent page and 20 interconnected databases that consolidate the schemas from 30+ individual plugins into a unified, standardized workspace architecture.\n\nThe workspace implements a hub-and-spoke model with the Companies database as the central CRM hub. All client-facing databases (Tasks, Meetings, Finance, Content, Deliverables, Research) can link back to Companies via relation properties. The Companies database directly incorporates P10 Client Health Dashboard scoring properties (Health Score, Health Status, Risk Flags with 24h cache TTL) and P20 Client Context Loader dossier properties (Dossier as JSON, Dossier Completeness 0.0-1.0, Dossier Generated At, Dossier Stale flag), eliminating the need for standalone health or dossier databases.\n\nMultiple databases use a Type column pattern to merge previously separate plugin databases while maintaining clear data origin tracking. For example, the Tasks database consolidates P01 email tasks, P04 action items, and P06 follow-ups using a Type column with values \"Action Item\", \"Follow-Up\", and \"Email Task\". Plugins can filter by Type when reading to maintain compatibility. Similar consolidation patterns appear in Finance (Invoice/Expense), Content (Newsletter/LinkedIn Post/Email Draft), Briefings (Daily Briefing/Weekly Review/Slack Digest/Morning Sync), Deliverables (Proposal/Contract/SOW), Research (Newsletter Research/Competitive Analysis), Knowledge Base (Source/Query), and Workflows (Execution/SOP).\n\nThe Meetings database implements a shared idempotent key pattern where P03 Meeting Prep Autopilot and P07 Meeting Intelligence Hub both use the Google Calendar Event ID to create or update the same record. This allows prep notes and post-meeting analysis to coexist in a single unified meeting record rather than separate databases.\n\nThe Goals ecosystem includes both Goals and Milestones databases with a parent-child relationship. Milestones link to parent Goals via relation property, and milestone completion drives automatic goal progress calculation. The system includes RAG status indicators (Green/Yellow/Red), velocity-based projected completion dates, and progress snapshot tracking for goals without milestones.\n\nThe Learnings ecosystem captures daily insights with auto-detected topics and cross-references to related past learnings. Weekly Insights database synthesizes weekly learning patterns with theme analysis, streak tracking (consecutive weeks with learnings), and trend comparison to previous weeks using 20% thresholds for \"More active\", \"Same pace\", or \"Less active\" classification.\n\nAll databases follow standardized discovery patterns where plugins first search for the HQ-prefixed name ([FOS] Companies), then fall back to legacy plugin-specific names (Companies, CRM - Companies). This enables gradual plugin migration to the HQ system while maintaining backward compatibility.\n\nThe deployment matches the founder-os-hq-template.json specification which defines detailed property schemas, default values, relation mappings, and plugin attribution notes for all 20 databases across the CRM, Operations, Intelligence, Content & Deliverables, and Growth & Meta sections.",

    "subtitle": "Complete specification for `npx founder-os@latest --init` command enabling one-command installation of founderOS into any project.",
    "facts": "[\"Spec [14] defines npm package `founder-os` with CLI entry point installing to `.founderOS/` (infrastructure) and `.claude/` (commands/skills/agents)\",\"Installation is idempotent using manifest.json with SHA-256 checksums to detect user modifications and skip overwriting customized files\",\"CLI supports --init (install/update), --version, --status, --uninstall, and --force flags for complete lifecycle management\",\"CLAUDE.md generation uses `<!-- founder-os:start -->` markers to separate managed content from user content, enabling safe updates\",\"settings.json uses deep-merge strategy preserving user configurations while adding founderOS MCP server configs\",\"Release pipeline defined: dev repo → template assembly → version bump → npm publish from founder-os-dist repo\",\"Technical constraints: Node.js >= 18, no runtime dependencies, ~200 line CLI, synchronous file operations, sub-5-second install\",\"Update flow compares checksums per-file: unchanged files overwritten, modified files skipped with warnings, deleted files marked as user-removed\"]",
    "narrative": "A comprehensive architectural specification for distributing founderOS via npm was designed. The system enables users to run `npx founder-os@latest --init` to install all 37 command namespaces, 12 agent teams, and infrastructure into their project. The design splits content into `.founderOS/` (engine: dispatcher, memory, intelligence, scripts) and `.claude/` (interface: commands, skills, agents, hooks, settings). A manifest.json file tracks all managed files with SHA-256 checksums, enabling intelligent updates that preserve user customizations. The CLI detects modifications by comparing checksums and skips overwriting user-edited files while updating unchanged ones. CLAUDE.md uses HTML comment markers to separate founderOS-managed documentation from user content. The settings.json merge strategy deep-merges configurations to add MCP servers without clobbering user settings. The release pipeline assembles templates from the dev repo, bumps versions, and publishes to npm. Edge cases are handled including partial installs, permission errors, name collisions (user files take precedence), and uninstalls with modifications (requires --force). Success criteria include sub-5-second install, preservation of all user modifications on update, and clean uninstall capability.",

    "facts": "[\"Design specification created at founderOS/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-scout-namespace-design.md documenting P34 Scout namespace\",\"Scout namespace automates discovery of Claude Code skills, MCP servers, GitHub repos, and CLI packages with security review\",\"Seven commands designed: scout:find (discovery), scout:install (sandboxed install), scout:catalog (browse), scout:review (security re-scan), scout:promote (move to native namespace), scout:remove (uninstall), scout:sources (configure)\",\"Skills-first search priority: Claude Code skills (priority 1) > MCP servers (priority 2) > GitHub repos (priority 3) > npm/PyPI CLI tools (priority 4)\",\"Security sandbox implements 6-point scan checking for prompt injection, secret exfiltration, overly broad tools, data leakage, supply chain risk, and permission escalation\",\"Three-layer caching strategy: catalog.json (zero-token lookup), Memory Engine (semantic recall ~200-500 tokens), web search (budget-controlled 1,000-2,000 tokens per call)\",\"Token cost estimates: cache hit ~500 tokens, fresh search ~8,000-12,000 tokens, full install with security review ~15,000-20,000 tokens\",\"Four skills defined: scout-research (search strategy), scout-security (security checklist), scout-integration (wrapper templates), scout-common (catalog schema and sandbox management)\",\"Generated wrapper commands immediately usable in P27 workflow YAML pipelines before promotion to native namespaces\",\"Graceful degradation: operates in catalog-only mode when WebSearch unavailable, optional --deep mode activates multi-agent team\"]",
    "narrative": "Comprehensive design specification created for the Scout namespace (P34), a new external tool and skill discovery system for founderOS. The design solves the problem of manual tool research and integration by automating discovery, security review, and wrapper generation for external solutions. The system follows a skills-first priority cascade per Anthropic guidance, checking Claude Code skills first (zero-install, native format), then MCP servers, GitHub repos, and finally CLI packages. Every discovered tool passes through a security sandbox with a 6-point scan covering prompt injection, secret exfiltration, overly broad permissions, data leakage, supply chain risks, and permission escalation.     "facts": "[\"Founder OS is single-plugin ecosystem with 32 namespaces across 4 pillars: Daily Work (email, meetings), Code Without Coding (reports, contracts), MCP & Integrations (Notion, Drive, Slack), Meta & Growth (workflows, ROI)\",\"Plugin manifest at `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` organizes commands, skills, and agents by namespace with pattern `/founder-os:<namespace>:<action>`\",\"7 agent team namespaces use 4 coordination patterns: pipeline (sequential), parallel_gathering (concurrent merge), pipeline_batch (batched sequential), competing_hypotheses (parallel proposals with synthesis)\",\"MCP stack: Notion CLI (21 namespaces), gws CLI for Gmail/Calendar/Drive (20 namespaces replacing deprecated Google MCP servers March 2026), Filesystem (8), Slack (2), WebSearch (1)\",\"Memory engine provides cross-namespace shared memory with local SQLite store at `.memory/memory.db` using HNSW indexing, synced to Notion `[FOS] Memory` database\",\"Notion HQ template consolidates 21 databases with CRM Companies as central hub, using 11 merged databases with Type field discriminators (Tasks merges Email Task/Action Item/Follow-Up, Briefings merges Daily/Weekly/Slack/Morning)\",\"Universal patterns enforce: HQ DB discovery sequence, Type column population for merged DBs, Company relation linking, business context loading from `_infrastructure/context/active/`, scheduling via `--schedule` flag for 9 namespaces, memory injection at command start and observation logging at end\",\"Notion is source of truth with specs synced from `[NaluForge] Projects & Tasks` database to local `docs/specs/` directory\",\"Documentation structure: `docs/getting-started/`, `docs/specs/`, `docs/plans/`, `docs/superpowers/`, `docs/agent-specs/`, `docs/reference/`, `docs/reports/`\"]",
    "narrative": "The CLAUDE.md file provides comprehensive documentation for Founder OS, a single-plugin AI automation ecosystem serving SMB founders through 32 namespaces organized across four pillars. The plugin manifest at `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` enables automatic discovery with commands (`/founder-os:<namespace>:<action>`), skills, and agents organized hierarchically. Seven namespaces implement agent teams using four distinct coordination patterns: pipeline for sequential processing (Inbox Zero, Report Gen), parallel_gathering for concurrent data collection with lead merge (Daily Briefing, Meeting Prep, Client Context), pipeline_batch for batched sequential workflows (Invoice Processor), and competing_hypotheses for parallel proposals with synthesis (SOW Generator). The technology stack layers five MCP integrations: Notion CLI serving 21 namespaces as CRM backbone, gws CLI replacing deprecated Google MCP servers in March 2026 to serve 20 namespaces with Gmail/Calendar/Drive access, Filesystem MCP for 8 namespaces, Slack MCP for 2 namespaces, and Web Search for competitive research. A cross-namespace memory engine maintains shared state using local SQLite storage with HNSW indexing at `.memory/memory.db`, syncing to Notion `[FOS] Memory` database, injecting top 5 relevant memories before command execution and logging observations afterward for pattern detection. The Notion HQ template consolidates 21 interconnected databases with CRM Companies as the central hub, implementing 11 merged databases that use Type field discriminators (Tasks combines Email Task/Action Item/Follow-Up, Briefings combines Daily/Weekly/Slack/Morning, Finance combines Invoice/Expense). Universal patterns govern all 32 namespaces: HQ DB discovery sequences search \"[FOS] [Name]\" then fallback patterns, Type column mandatory for merged database writes, Company relations populated when client context available, business context loaded from `_infrastructure/context/active/`, scheduling support via `--schedule` flag for 9 namespaces generating P27 workflows, and memory integration with context injection and observation logging. Notion serves as source of truth with specs synced from `[NaluForge] Projects & Tasks` database to local `docs/specs/` directory.",

    "title": "Founder OS Single Plugin Architecture Specification",
    "subtitle": "Formalized restructuring design moving from 33 separate plugins to unified directory-based plugin with auto-discovery",
    "facts": "[\"Design specification documents transformation of 33 founder-os-* plugins into single unified founder-os plugin at repository root\",\"New architecture uses directory-based namespacing: commands/namespace/action.md resolves to /founder-os:namespace:action\",\"Plugin manifest reduced from 33 separate plugin.json files to single .claude-plugin/plugin.json with minimal metadata\",\"MCP configuration consolidated from 33 separate .mcp.json files to single root-level configuration\",\"Installation process simplified by removing symlink phase entirely - repo becomes the plugin directly\",\"32 namespaces defined: setup, inbox, briefing, prep, actions, review, followup, meeting, newsletter, report, health, invoice, proposal, contract, sow, compete, expense, notion, drive, slack, client, crm, morning, kb, linkedin, savings, prompt, workflow, workflow-doc, learn, goal, memory, intel\",\"Grouped directory pattern applied consistently across commands/, skills/, and agents/ directories\",\"Future feature additions require only creating new namespace directories - no manifest or installation script updates needed\"]",
    "narrative": "Created comprehensive design specification for restructuring Founder OS from 33 independent plugins to a single unified Claude Code plugin. The current architecture uses symlinks to .claude/plugins/ which Claude Code ignores (that directory is for marketplace-installed plugins only), causing commands, skills, and agents to be invisible after installation. The approved design makes the repository itself the plugin by placing .claude-plugin/plugin.json at the root and organizing all components in standard directories that Claude Code automatically scans. Commands are organized into 32 namespace subdirectories under commands/, with filenames stripped of namespace prefixes (inbox-triage.md becomes inbox/triage.md). This creates a three-tier namespace hierarchy (/founder-os:namespace:action) instead of the current two-tier system. The specification includes complete directory structure showing all 94 commands, plugin manifest format relying on auto-discovery, command naming resolution table, namespace mapping for all plugin origins, single consolidated MCP configuration, simplified 6-phase installation process (removing symlink management), release script changes, command file migration requirements, future extensibility model, and cleanup list of what gets removed. The design prioritizes simplicity, maintainability, and automatic discovery - reducing installation complexity, enabling future additions with just directory creation, and consolidating 99+ documentation files into root-level docs. This represents the culmination of architectural decisions about namespacing, grouping patterns, and organizational structure.",

    "type": "decision",
    "title": "Adaptive Intelligence Engine Architecture Specification",
    "subtitle": "Designed hooks-first intelligence layer enabling plugins to learn preferences, self-heal from errors, and gradually increase autonomy through confidence-gated pattern application",
    "facts": "[\"Architecture separates infrastructure layer (_infrastructure/intelligence/) from user-facing Intelligence Hub plugin (founder-os-intelligence-hub/)\",\"Three core modules implemented: Hooks (event capture), Learning (observe-retrieve-judge-distill-consolidate-apply cycle), Self-Healing (error classification with retry/degradation)\",\"Learning system operates in three tiers: Tier 1 taste-learning (output preferences), Tier 2 workflow-optimization (command chain detection), Tier 3 confidence-gating (graduated autonomy)\",\"Hook system captures five event types: pre_command, post_command, mcp_call, decision_point, error stored in SQLite with 30-day retention\",\"Pattern confidence calculated as confirmations / (confirmations + rejections * 2), auto-applied at confidence >= 0.5 with 3+ observations\",\"Error classification uses four categories: Transient (retry with backoff), Recoverable (fix + retry), Degradable (fallback + continue), Fatal (stop + notify)\",\"Six slash commands provide user control: /intel:status, /intel:patterns, /intel:approve, /intel:reset, /intel:healing, /intel:config\",\"Patterns sync bidirectionally with Notion database containing confidence scores, observation counts, and injectable instructions\",\"Plugin integration uses convention-based observation blocks rather than requiring framework dependencies\",\"Architecture adapted from ruflo/claude-flow v3.5 SONA system, modified for single-execution plugin commands instead of multi-agent swarms\"]",
    "narrative": "Created comprehensive design specification for an Adaptive Intelligence Engine that adds learning and self-healing capabilities to the Founder OS plugin ecosystem. The architecture uses a hooks-first approach with a separate behavioral layer built on top of the existing Memory Engine. The infrastructure layer provides four modules (hooks, learning, self-healing, routing) backed by a dedicated SQLite database, while a user-facing Intelligence Hub plugin exposes control through six slash commands. The hooks system captures structured events at command-level, MCP-level, and decision-point-level, storing them with session context for pattern analysis. The learning system implements a retrieve-judge-distill-consolidate-route cycle that observes plugin executions, detects repeated patterns, calculates confidence scores based on user confirmations vs rejections, and injects learned preferences as instructions into future runs. Learning operates in three graduated tiers: taste-learning for output preferences (ships first), workflow-optimization for command chain detection, and confidence-gating for autonomous decision-making. The self-healing module classifies errors into four categories with distinct handling strategies—transient errors retry with exponential backoff, recoverable errors apply known fixes, degradable errors execute fallback paths, and fatal errors stop with clear user guidance. A fallback registry ships with seed data for all 30 plugins defining graceful degradation paths. The system tracks healing effectiveness and reclassifies error categories over time. Patterns sync bidirectionally with a Notion database, allowing users to review and edit learned behaviors outside the CLI. The specification includes complete database schemas, confidence mechanics, plugin integration conventions, and a seven-phase ship order prioritizing immediate observability value through hooks before rolling out adaptive features. Architecture inspired by ruflo/claude-flow v3.5 but adapted for single-execution plugin commands rather than long-running multi-agent swarms.",

    "narrative": "Deployed a complete Founder OS HQ Notion workspace to the user's Notion account with [FOS] prefix for namespace identification. The deployment created 1 parent page and 20 interconnected databases that consolidate the schemas from 30+ individual plugins into a unified, standardized workspace architecture.\n\nThe workspace implements a hub-and-spoke model with the Companies database as the central CRM hub. All client-facing databases (Tasks, Meetings, Finance, Content, Deliverables, Research) can link back to Companies via relation properties. The Companies database directly incorporates P10 Client Health Dashboard scoring properties (Health Score, Health Status, Risk Flags with 24h cache TTL) and P20 Client Context Loader dossier properties (Dossier as JSON, Dossier Completeness 0.0-1.0, Dossier Generated At, Dossier Stale flag), eliminating the need for standalone health or dossier databases.\n\nMultiple databases use a Type column pattern to merge previously separate plugin databases while maintaining clear data origin tracking. For example, the Tasks database consolidates P01 email tasks, P04 action items, and P06 follow-ups using a Type column with values \"Action Item\", \"Follow-Up\", and \"Email Task\". Plugins can filter by Type when reading to maintain compatibility. Similar consolidation patterns appear in Finance (Invoice/Expense), Content (Newsletter/LinkedIn Post/Email Draft), Briefings (Daily Briefing/Weekly Review/Slack Digest/Morning Sync), Deliverables (Proposal/Contract/SOW), Research (Newsletter Research/Competitive Analysis), Knowledge Base (Source/Query), and Workflows (Execution/SOP).\n\nThe Meetings database implements a shared idempotent key pattern where P03 Meeting Prep Autopilot and P07 Meeting Intelligence Hub both use the Google Calendar Event ID to create or update the same record. This allows prep notes and post-meeting analysis to coexist in a single unified meeting record rather than separate databases.\n\nThe Goals ecosystem includes both Goals and Milestones databases with a parent-child relationship. Milestones link to parent Goals via relation property, and milestone completion drives automatic goal progress calculation. The system includes RAG status indicators (Green/Yellow/Red), velocity-based projected completion dates, and progress snapshot tracking for goals without milestones.\n\nThe Learnings ecosystem captures daily insights with auto-detected topics and cross-references to related past learnings. Weekly Insights database synthesizes weekly learning patterns with theme analysis, streak tracking (consecutive weeks with learnings), and trend comparison to previous weeks using 20% thresholds for \"More active\", \"Same pace\", or \"Less active\" classification.\n\nAll databases follow standardized discovery patterns where plugins first search for the HQ-prefixed name ([FOS] Companies), then fall back to legacy plugin-specific names (Companies, CRM - Companies). This enables gradual plugin migration to the HQ system while maintaining backward compatibility.\n\nThe deployment matches the founder-os-hq-template.json specification which defines detailed property schemas, default values, relation mappings, and plugin attribution notes for all 20 databases across the CRM, Operations, Intelligence, Content & Deliverables, and Growth & Meta sections.",

input.content:
# Founder OS: The Complete Development Timeline

> A chronological record of building an AI-native operating system for solo founders — from first commit to distribution-ready product. This document serves as the canonical source for "Build in Public" content across blog posts, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, short-form video (Reels/TikTok), and long-form storytelling.

---

## The Origin Story (Late January 2026)

### The Problem
Solo founders drown in operational overhead — email triage, CRM updates, follow-ups, proposals, invoices, content creation, competitive intel. Every hour spent on admin is an hour not spent building. What if an AI could handle all of it?

### The Spark: LinkedIn Outreach Agents (Jan 30, 2026)
The journey started not with Founder OS itself, but with a set of **LinkedIn automation agents** built as standalone Python projects:

- **Lead Enrichment Agent** — Extracts LinkedIn profile data and post context using MCP browser tools. Clean architecture with an `EnrichmentManager` orchestrator pattern.
- **Ice Breaker Agent** — Generates personalized outreach messages by analyzing a prospect's recent activity.
- **LinkedIn Contact Finder** — Dual-mode prospecting system (manual + Apify API) for building targeted prospect lists.

These three agents were designed as separate codebases with independent backlogs, using a manager-based architecture pattern. They integrated with Notion for CRM data storage and used browser automation via MCP tools.

**Key Insight:** While building these agents, the realization hit — every founder needs a *suite* of AI agents, not just one. This planted the seed for Founder OS.

### First Architecture Decisions (Jan 30 - Feb 1, 2026)
- Chose **Beads** as the issue tracking system — git-backed, persistent across AI conversation compaction.
- Established a phased planning approach: Discovery → Codebase Exploration → Clarifying Questions → Architecture Design → Implementation → Beads Backlog.
- Discovered the **Superpowers Framework** (14 skills for software development workflows) and the parallel agent dispatching pattern that would become core to the build process.

---

## Phase 1: The 30-Plugin Vision (Feb 11 - Feb 14, 2026)

### Blueprint Day (Feb 11, 2026)
This was the day the full scope crystallized. The Notion workspace was set up with a comprehensive project tracking system:

- **30 plugins** designed across **4 pillars**:
  - Pillar 1: Daily Work (email, calendar, follow-ups, briefings)
  - Pillar 2: Code Without Coding (proposals, SOWs, contracts, reports)
  - Pillar 3: MCP & Integrations (Notion, Google Drive, LinkedIn, CRM)
  - Pillar 4: Meta & Growth (learning logs, goal tracking, time savings, competitive intel)
- **5 priority plugins** identified for Weeks 1-5 initial development.
- Master specification document created defining all 30 plugins with acceptance criteria.

The project structure was established in Notion with two databases (Projects and Tasks), and the CLAUDE.md was written to define architecture, development standards, and agent-driven plugin development workflows.

### Plugin #01: Inbox Zero Commander (Feb 13, 2026)
The first plugin built from scratch. This was the proving ground for the entire development workflow:

- **Multiple false starts** — the initial agent implementation was over-engineered. A complete project reset was performed, removing all implementation code and clearing the backlog for a fresh start.
- **Pipeline agent pattern** emerged as the core architecture: a triage agent classifies emails, an action agent creates Notion tasks.
- Final implementation included 10 integration test scenarios, complete README documentation, and v1.0.0 plugin manifest.
- Created the `/build-plugin` and `/plan-plugin` custom commands to make the plugin development process repeatable.

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"I threw away my first attempt completely. The temptation to over-engineer an AI agent is real. The second attempt — simple pipeline, clear boundaries — shipped in a fraction of the time."*

### Client Context Loader (Feb 14, 2026)
The second plugin shipped, establishing the pattern: fetch Notion spec → explore codebase → design architecture → build → validate. The plugin scaffold template was formalized with metadata structure, README template, and test plan template.

---

## Phase 2: The Plugin Factory (Feb 23 - Mar 7, 2026)

### Assembly Line Mode (Feb 23 - Mar 6, 2026)
With the workflow proven, development entered a cadence. Plugins shipped rapidly:

| Date | Plugin | Pillar | Notable |
|------|--------|--------|---------|
| Feb 23 | P09 SOW Generator | 2 | Pricing agent with competing-hypotheses pattern |
| Feb 23 | Invoice Processor | 2 | Financial document automation |
| Feb 25 | Daily Briefing Generator | 1 | Cross-source morning summary |
| Feb 27 | P04 Action Item Extractor | 1 | Meeting → task pipeline |
| Feb 27 | P06 Follow-Up Tracker | 1 | Chained plugin dependencies |
| Feb 28 | P08 Newsletter Draft Engine | 2 | Multi-source research pipeline |
| Feb 28 | P10 Client Health Dashboard | 1 | RAG status tracking |
| Feb 28 | P15 Competitive Intel Compiler | 2 | Web research aggregation |
| Mar 1 | P03 CRM Sync Hub | 3 | Three-MCP-server integration |
| Mar 4 | P17 Notion Command Center | 3 | Database template management |
| Mar 4 | P23 Knowledge Base Q&A | 3 | Semantic document search |
| Mar 4 | P05 Weekly Review Compiler | 1 | Cross-plugin data aggregation |
| Mar 5 | P12 Proposal Automator | 2 | Section templates, pricing strategy skill |
| Mar 5 | P16 Expense Report Builder | 2 | Categorization engine |
| Mar 5 | P24 LinkedIn Post Generator | 3 | 7 writing frameworks, founder voice |
| Mar 5 | P07 Transcription Aggregator | 3 | Redesigned from transcript generator |
| Mar 5 | P22 Workflow Documenter | 2 | Process capture automation |
| Mar 6 | P25 Time Savings Calculator | 4 | ROI measurement |
| Mar 6 | P26 Team Prompt Library | 4 | Quality-gated prompt management |
| Mar 7 | P27 Workflow Automator | 4 | Three-skill decomposition |
| Mar 7 | P29 Learning Log Tracker | 4 | Knowledge retention system |
| Mar 7 | P30 Goal Progress Tracker | 4 | Milestone tracking, Gantt generation |

**Key Pattern:** Each plugin followed the same lifecycle: Notion spec → Beads backlog → Plugin scaffold → Skills + Commands → Integration tests → README + INSTALL docs.

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"30 plugins in 3 weeks. Not by coding faster — by building a repeatable system. The /build-plugin command turned plugin creation into a factory."*

### The Notion HQ Consolidation (Mar 7, 2026)
A pivotal architectural decision. The 30 plugins had created **32 separate Notion database templates**. This was unsustainable for users. The solution:

- Consolidated into **20 interconnected databases** under a single "Founder OS HQ" workspace.
- Created a comprehensive HQ template JSON with cross-database relations.
- Updated all plugin INSTALL docs to use HQ database discovery patterns.
- Built test data preparation plan with 6 sequential phases of CRM, content, and deliverable records.

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"We had 32 databases. Users would need a database to manage their databases. The consolidation into 20 interconnected databases under one HQ was painful but necessary."*

---

## Phase 3: The Platform Emerges (Mar 3 - Mar 9, 2026)

### OpenViber: The Web Application Layer (Mar 3 - Mar 7, 2026)
Parallel to plugin development, a web application was being built:

- **Backend:** Python/FastAPI with multi-tenant architecture (Alembic migrations, tenant schema isolation, audit trails)
- **Frontend:** Next.js 15 with Zustand state management, CopilotKit AI integration
- **Infrastructure:** Docker Compose for local development, Celery for background tasks
- **Security:** Multiple hardening PRs (routing security, database triggers, transaction safety, CORS, rate limiting)
- **Tier System:** T0 (Repository Setup) → T1 (Foundation/Auth) → T2 (Core UX) → T3 (Integrations) → T4 (Agent Factory) → T5 (Deployment) → T6 (Monitoring)
- Tier 0 and Tier 1 fully delivered by March 4. Auth, multi-tenant services, and API merged.

### The NaluForge Landing Page (Mar 11, 2026)
Built the marketing site in a single day using Vite + React with Framer Motion animations:

- Brand design tokens established (color hierarchy, two-font typography)
- Scroll-reactive header with progress indicator
- Sections: Hero, Examples, Pricing, WhatYouGet, Footer
- Deployed to now.naluforge.com on Hostinger
- Full design review cycle using browser automation tools

### UAT Testing: Proving It Works (Mar 8 - Mar 11, 2026)
Comprehensive User Acceptance Testing across all 30 plugins:

- Created UAT testing framework with plugin installation scripts (symlink-based)
- Built email test fixtures (newsletters, client communications, follow-up chains)
- CSV data fixtures for client satisfaction analysis
- **Results:**
  - Pillar 1 (Daily Work): **96.4% pass rate** across 8 plugins
  - Pillar 2 (Code Without Coding): **100% pass rate** after resolving 7 initial failures
  - Phase 6 stress testing (meeting marathon scenario)
  - Discovered and fixed database name mismatches, schema gaps
- Test results tracked in Notion via dedicated Test Results database

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"We didn't just build 30 plugins — we tested every single one. 96.4% first-pass rate on Pillar 1. The 3.6% that failed? Database naming inconsistencies. The boring bugs are always the real ones."*

---

## Phase 4: Architecture Revolution (Mar 11 - Mar 12, 2026)

### The Single Plugin Restructure (Mar 11, 2026)
The biggest architectural pivot. The 30 separate plugin directories were creating maintenance nightmares:

- **Before:** 30 independent plugin directories, each with their own `.claude-plugin/`, `.mcp.json`, skills, commands
- **After:** Single unified plugin with namespace-based organization (commands grouped by domain: `inbox/`, `proposal/`, `goal/`, `review/`, etc.)
- 97 skills migrated to namespace structure
- Release script updated for the new distribution model

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"30 plugins sounds impressive. One plugin with 30 capabilities is actually usable. We restructured the entire codebase in a day."*

### Marketplace Distribution Pivot (Mar 11, 2026)
Originally planned as a custom `install.sh` script. Pivoted to Claude Code marketplace integration:

- Discovered Claude Code's marketplace directory structure (`~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/`)
- Created `marketplace.json` for plugin registry
- Updated setup guide from install script to marketplace architecture
- This pivot eliminated the need for a custom installer entirely

### The Adaptive Intelligence Engine (Mar 11, 2026)
The system that makes Founder OS learn from usage:

- **Hooks Module:** Pre/post-command observation blocks wired into all 82 commands
- **Learning Module:** Taste-learning, confidence-gating, workflow-optimization skills
- **Self-Healing Module:** Fallback registry for graceful degradation when integrations fail
- **Notion Intelligence Database:** Tracks learning events and adaptation patterns
- Pilot integration with Inbox Zero and Adaptive Intel plugins

---

## Phase 5: Superpowers Era (Mar 12 - Mar 16, 2026)

### The Superpowers Framework
A formal design-then-implement workflow was adopted. Each "superpower" gets a numbered design specification, goes through review, then gets an implementation plan:

| # | Superpower | Status | Date |
|---|-----------|--------|------|
| [1] | AIOS Enrichment | Done | Mar 11 |
| [2] | Memory Engine | Done | Mar 11 |
| [3] | Intelligence Engine | Done | Mar 11-14 |
| [4] | Installer/Distribution | Done | Mar 11 |
| [5] | Single Plugin Restructure | Done | Mar 11 |
| [6] | Notion CLI Migration | Done | Mar 12 |
| [7] | Self-Learning Loop | Designed | Mar 12 |
| [8] | Agent Factory Scaffold | Designed | Mar 12 |
| [9] | Evals & Quality Framework | Done | Mar 14 |
| [10] | Social Media Posting | Done | Mar 13-14 |
| [11] | Humanize Content | Done | Mar 14 |
| [12] | Scout Namespace | Done | Mar 14-15 |
| [13] | Preflight Dependencies | Done | Mar 13 |
| [14] | NPM Distribution | Done | Mar 15 |
| [15] | Social Template Engine | Done | Mar 16 |
| [16] | Content Ideation Refactor | Done | Mar 16 |

### Memory Engine (Superpower [2])
Shared memory system across all plugins with Notion sync. 18 tasks completed, enabling plugins to learn from each other's outputs.

### Evals & Quality Framework (Superpower [9], Mar 14, 2026)
Built a comprehensive quality evaluation system:

- Universal evaluation rubric (JSON-based scoring)
- Namespace-specific rubric overrides (e.g., inbox gets email quality checks)
- Eval runner orchestration engine (`eval-runner.mjs`)
- Telemetry and sampling modules for continuous quality monitoring
- Humanize-content evaluation rubric integrated

### Scout Namespace (Superpower [12], Mar 14-15, 2026)
A new capability: find, evaluate, and integrate Claude Code plugins from the ecosystem:

- `scout:find` — Search for plugins matching a need
- `scout:catalog` — Browse available plugins
- `scout:install` — Integration with sandbox isolation
- Security skill implementing 6-point advisory scan
- Domain-specific skills for evaluation criteria

### Humanize Content (Superpower [11], Mar 14, 2026)
Making AI-generated content sound human:

- Core humanization pipeline skill
- Banned vocabulary reference (overused AI phrases)
- LinkedIn-specific rules and constraints
- Wired into the content creation commands

### Social Media Posting (Superpower [10], Mar 13-14, 2026)
Integration with Late.dev API for actual social media publishing:

- Platform adaptation skill with constraint references (character limits, formatting rules)
- `social:post`, `social:reply`, `social:compose` commands
- Media upload workflow infrastructure
- Content database schema extended for social media types

---

## Phase 6: Content Creation System (Mar 15 - Mar 16, 2026)

### Social Template Engine (Superpower [15], Mar 16, 2026)
Discovered **18 months of social media post templates** stored in Notion — a goldmine of proven content patterns:

- Imported 29+ templates covering March-August 2024 period
- Templates categorized by rhetorical technique:
  - Contrarian hooks ("Contrary to popular belief...")
  - Anaphora patterns ("Embrace discomfort...")
  - Reversal frameworks ("Old Era vs New Era")
  - Skill-to-business step formats
  - High-performer pivot narratives
- Template engine designed with JSON-based format for structure and metadata

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"18 months of LinkedIn posts, analyzed and turned into reusable templates. Every post I've ever written is now a template my AI can remix."*

### Content Ideation Namespace Refactor (Superpower [16], Mar 16, 2026)
The LinkedIn-specific namespace was limiting. Refactored to a platform-agnostic `ideate` namespace:

- `ideate:draft` — Generate content for any platform
- `ideate:from-doc` — Transform documents into social content
- `ideate:variations` — Create platform-specific variations
- `ideate:hooks` — Generate attention-grabbing openers
- Platform calibration via founder-voice skill (different tone for LinkedIn vs Twitter vs TikTok)
- Universal execution flow supporting multi-platform output from single input

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"We started with 'LinkedIn posts.' We ended with 'content ideation for any platform.' The abstraction was obvious in hindsight."*

---

## Phase 7: Distribution & Packaging (Mar 15 - Mar 16, 2026)

### NPM Distribution System (Superpower [14], Mar 15, 2026)
Making Founder OS installable with a single command:

- Created `founder-os` npm package with MIT license
- `npx founder-os --init` scaffolds a complete workspace
- Release script with path rewriting and template assembly
- Package.json with `files` field controlling distribution contents
- Separate distribution repository (`founder-os-dist`)

### Installer Improvements (Mar 16, 2026)
- CLAUDE.md now generates in project root (not `.claude/` subdirectory)
- Environment file scaffolding: `.env`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`
- `.env.example` documents all API keys (Notion, Google Workspace, Late.dev)
- Comprehensive `.gitignore` with security best practices
- Consistent handling across install, update, and uninstall lifecycle
- Marker-based CLAUDE.md merge system preserves user customizations

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"One command. npx founder-os --init. That's all it takes to go from empty directory to AI-powered founder workspace."*

---

## Key Themes for Content Creation

### 1. "The Factory, Not The Product"
The real innovation isn't any single plugin — it's the system for creating plugins. The `/build-plugin` command, the scaffold templates, the phased planning approach. This is repeatable.

### 2. "Reset and Restart"
The Feb 13 reset (throwing away the first Inbox Zero implementation) and the Mar 11 single-plugin restructure both demonstrate that the courage to start over produces better results.

### 3. "30 Plugins in 30 Days"
The cadence of shipping — from concept to working plugin — using AI-assisted development with structured workflows.

### 4. "AI Building AI Tools"
The meta-narrative: Claude Code is being used to build a tool that makes Claude Code more useful for founders. The Superpowers framework, the intelligence engine, the self-learning loop.

toolUseResult.content:
# Founder OS: The Complete Development Timeline

> A chronological record of building an AI-native operating system for solo founders — from first commit to distribution-ready product. This document serves as the canonical source for "Build in Public" content across blog posts, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, short-form video (Reels/TikTok), and long-form storytelling.

---

## The Origin Story (Late January 2026)

### The Problem
Solo founders drown in operational overhead — email triage, CRM updates, follow-ups, proposals, invoices, content creation, competitive intel. Every hour spent on admin is an hour not spent building. What if an AI could handle all of it?

### The Spark: LinkedIn Outreach Agents (Jan 30, 2026)
The journey started not with Founder OS itself, but with a set of **LinkedIn automation agents** built as standalone Python projects:

- **Lead Enrichment Agent** — Extracts LinkedIn profile data and post context using MCP browser tools. Clean architecture with an `EnrichmentManager` orchestrator pattern.
- **Ice Breaker Agent** — Generates personalized outreach messages by analyzing a prospect's recent activity.
- **LinkedIn Contact Finder** — Dual-mode prospecting system (manual + Apify API) for building targeted prospect lists.

These three agents were designed as separate codebases with independent backlogs, using a manager-based architecture pattern. They integrated with Notion for CRM data storage and used browser automation via MCP tools.

**Key Insight:** While building these agents, the realization hit — every founder needs a *suite* of AI agents, not just one. This planted the seed for Founder OS.

### First Architecture Decisions (Jan 30 - Feb 1, 2026)
- Chose **Beads** as the issue tracking system — git-backed, persistent across AI conversation compaction.
- Established a phased planning approach: Discovery → Codebase Exploration → Clarifying Questions → Architecture Design → Implementation → Beads Backlog.
- Discovered the **Superpowers Framework** (14 skills for software development workflows) and the parallel agent dispatching pattern that would become core to the build process.

---

## Phase 1: The 30-Plugin Vision (Feb 11 - Feb 14, 2026)

### Blueprint Day (Feb 11, 2026)
This was the day the full scope crystallized. The Notion workspace was set up with a comprehensive project tracking system:

- **30 plugins** designed across **4 pillars**:
  - Pillar 1: Daily Work (email, calendar, follow-ups, briefings)
  - Pillar 2: Code Without Coding (proposals, SOWs, contracts, reports)
  - Pillar 3: MCP & Integrations (Notion, Google Drive, LinkedIn, CRM)
  - Pillar 4: Meta & Growth (learning logs, goal tracking, time savings, competitive intel)
- **5 priority plugins** identified for Weeks 1-5 initial development.
- Master specification document created defining all 30 plugins with acceptance criteria.

The project structure was established in Notion with two databases (Projects and Tasks), and the CLAUDE.md was written to define architecture, development standards, and agent-driven plugin development workflows.

### Plugin #01: Inbox Zero Commander (Feb 13, 2026)
The first plugin built from scratch. This was the proving ground for the entire development workflow:

- **Multiple false starts** — the initial agent implementation was over-engineered. A complete project reset was performed, removing all implementation code and clearing the backlog for a fresh start.
- **Pipeline agent pattern** emerged as the core architecture: a triage agent classifies emails, an action agent creates Notion tasks.
- Final implementation included 10 integration test scenarios, complete README documentation, and v1.0.0 plugin manifest.
- Created the `/build-plugin` and `/plan-plugin` custom commands to make the plugin development process repeatable.

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"I threw away my first attempt completely. The temptation to over-engineer an AI agent is real. The second attempt — simple pipeline, clear boundaries — shipped in a fraction of the time."*

### Client Context Loader (Feb 14, 2026)
The second plugin shipped, establishing the pattern: fetch Notion spec → explore codebase → design architecture → build → validate. The plugin scaffold template was formalized with metadata structure, README template, and test plan template.

---

## Phase 2: The Plugin Factory (Feb 23 - Mar 7, 2026)

### Assembly Line Mode (Feb 23 - Mar 6, 2026)
With the workflow proven, development entered a cadence. Plugins shipped rapidly:

| Date | Plugin | Pillar | Notable |
|------|--------|--------|---------|
| Feb 23 | P09 SOW Generator | 2 | Pricing agent with competing-hypotheses pattern |
| Feb 23 | Invoice Processor | 2 | Financial document automation |
| Feb 25 | Daily Briefing Generator | 1 | Cross-source morning summary |
| Feb 27 | P04 Action Item Extractor | 1 | Meeting → task pipeline |
| Feb 27 | P06 Follow-Up Tracker | 1 | Chained plugin dependencies |
| Feb 28 | P08 Newsletter Draft Engine | 2 | Multi-source research pipeline |
| Feb 28 | P10 Client Health Dashboard | 1 | RAG status tracking |
| Feb 28 | P15 Competitive Intel Compiler | 2 | Web research aggregation |
| Mar 1 | P03 CRM Sync Hub | 3 | Three-MCP-server integration |
| Mar 4 | P17 Notion Command Center | 3 | Database template management |
| Mar 4 | P23 Knowledge Base Q&A | 3 | Semantic document search |
| Mar 4 | P05 Weekly Review Compiler | 1 | Cross-plugin data aggregation |
| Mar 5 | P12 Proposal Automator | 2 | Section templates, pricing strategy skill |
| Mar 5 | P16 Expense Report Builder | 2 | Categorization engine |
| Mar 5 | P24 LinkedIn Post Generator | 3 | 7 writing frameworks, founder voice |
| Mar 5 | P07 Transcription Aggregator | 3 | Redesigned from transcript generator |
| Mar 5 | P22 Workflow Documenter | 2 | Process capture automation |
| Mar 6 | P25 Time Savings Calculator | 4 | ROI measurement |
| Mar 6 | P26 Team Prompt Library | 4 | Quality-gated prompt management |
| Mar 7 | P27 Workflow Automator | 4 | Three-skill decomposition |
| Mar 7 | P29 Learning Log Tracker | 4 | Knowledge retention system |
| Mar 7 | P30 Goal Progress Tracker | 4 | Milestone tracking, Gantt generation |

**Key Pattern:** Each plugin followed the same lifecycle: Notion spec → Beads backlog → Plugin scaffold → Skills + Commands → Integration tests → README + INSTALL docs.

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"30 plugins in 3 weeks. Not by coding faster — by building a repeatable system. The /build-plugin command turned plugin creation into a factory."*

### The Notion HQ Consolidation (Mar 7, 2026)
A pivotal architectural decision. The 30 plugins had created **32 separate Notion database templates**. This was unsustainable for users. The solution:

- Consolidated into **20 interconnected databases** under a single "Founder OS HQ" workspace.
- Created a comprehensive HQ template JSON with cross-database relations.
- Updated all plugin INSTALL docs to use HQ database discovery patterns.
- Built test data preparation plan with 6 sequential phases of CRM, content, and deliverable records.

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"We had 32 databases. Users would need a database to manage their databases. The consolidation into 20 interconnected databases under one HQ was painful but necessary."*

---

## Phase 3: The Platform Emerges (Mar 3 - Mar 9, 2026)

### OpenViber: The Web Application Layer (Mar 3 - Mar 7, 2026)
Parallel to plugin development, a web application was being built:

- **Backend:** Python/FastAPI with multi-tenant architecture (Alembic migrations, tenant schema isolation, audit trails)
- **Frontend:** Next.js 15 with Zustand state management, CopilotKit AI integration
- **Infrastructure:** Docker Compose for local development, Celery for background tasks
- **Security:** Multiple hardening PRs (routing security, database triggers, transaction safety, CORS, rate limiting)
- **Tier System:** T0 (Repository Setup) → T1 (Foundation/Auth) → T2 (Core UX) → T3 (Integrations) → T4 (Agent Factory) → T5 (Deployment) → T6 (Monitoring)
- Tier 0 and Tier 1 fully delivered by March 4. Auth, multi-tenant services, and API merged.

### The NaluForge Landing Page (Mar 11, 2026)
Built the marketing site in a single day using Vite + React with Framer Motion animations:

- Brand design tokens established (color hierarchy, two-font typography)
- Scroll-reactive header with progress indicator
- Sections: Hero, Examples, Pricing, WhatYouGet, Footer
- Deployed to now.naluforge.com on Hostinger
- Full design review cycle using browser automation tools

### UAT Testing: Proving It Works (Mar 8 - Mar 11, 2026)
Comprehensive User Acceptance Testing across all 30 plugins:

- Created UAT testing framework with plugin installation scripts (symlink-based)
- Built email test fixtures (newsletters, client communications, follow-up chains)
- CSV data fixtures for client satisfaction analysis
- **Results:**
  - Pillar 1 (Daily Work): **96.4% pass rate** across 8 plugins
  - Pillar 2 (Code Without Coding): **100% pass rate** after resolving 7 initial failures
  - Phase 6 stress testing (meeting marathon scenario)
  - Discovered and fixed database name mismatches, schema gaps
- Test results tracked in Notion via dedicated Test Results database

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"We didn't just build 30 plugins — we tested every single one. 96.4% first-pass rate on Pillar 1. The 3.6% that failed? Database naming inconsistencies. The boring bugs are always the real ones."*

---

## Phase 4: Architecture Revolution (Mar 11 - Mar 12, 2026)

### The Single Plugin Restructure (Mar 11, 2026)
The biggest architectural pivot. The 30 separate plugin directories were creating maintenance nightmares:

- **Before:** 30 independent plugin directories, each with their own `.claude-plugin/`, `.mcp.json`, skills, commands
- **After:** Single unified plugin with namespace-based organization (commands grouped by domain: `inbox/`, `proposal/`, `goal/`, `review/`, etc.)
- 97 skills migrated to namespace structure
- Release script updated for the new distribution model

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"30 plugins sounds impressive. One plugin with 30 capabilities is actually usable. We restructured the entire codebase in a day."*

### Marketplace Distribution Pivot (Mar 11, 2026)
Originally planned as a custom `install.sh` script. Pivoted to Claude Code marketplace integration:

- Discovered Claude Code's marketplace directory structure (`~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/`)
- Created `marketplace.json` for plugin registry
- Updated setup guide from install script to marketplace architecture
- This pivot eliminated the need for a custom installer entirely

### The Adaptive Intelligence Engine (Mar 11, 2026)
The system that makes Founder OS learn from usage:

- **Hooks Module:** Pre/post-command observation blocks wired into all 82 commands
- **Learning Module:** Taste-learning, confidence-gating, workflow-optimization skills
- **Self-Healing Module:** Fallback registry for graceful degradation when integrations fail
- **Notion Intelligence Database:** Tracks learning events and adaptation patterns
- Pilot integration with Inbox Zero and Adaptive Intel plugins

---

## Phase 5: Superpowers Era (Mar 12 - Mar 16, 2026)

### The Superpowers Framework
A formal design-then-implement workflow was adopted. Each "superpower" gets a numbered design specification, goes through review, then gets an implementation plan:

| # | Superpower | Status | Date |
|---|-----------|--------|------|
| [1] | AIOS Enrichment | Done | Mar 11 |
| [2] | Memory Engine | Done | Mar 11 |
| [3] | Intelligence Engine | Done | Mar 11-14 |
| [4] | Installer/Distribution | Done | Mar 11 |
| [5] | Single Plugin Restructure | Done | Mar 11 |

| [10] | Social Media Posting | Done | Mar 13-14 |

| [13] | Preflight Dependencies | Done | Mar 13 |
| [14] | NPM Distribution | Done | Mar 15 |

| [16] | Content Ideation Refactor | Done | Mar 16 |

### Memory Engine (Superpower [2])
Shared memory system across all plugins with Notion sync. 18 tasks completed, enabling plugins to learn from each other's outputs.

### Evals & Quality Framework (Superpower [9], Mar 14, 2026)
Built a comprehensive quality evaluation system:

- Universal evaluation rubric (JSON-based scoring)
- Namespace-specific rubric overrides (e.g., inbox gets email quality checks)
- Eval runner orchestration engine (`eval-runner.mjs`)
- Telemetry and sampling modules for continuous quality monitoring
- Humanize-content evaluation rubric integrated

### Scout Namespace (Superpower [12], Mar 14-15, 2026)
A new capability: find, evaluate, and integrate Claude Code plugins from the ecosystem:

- `scout:find` — Search for plugins matching a need
- `scout:catalog` — Browse available plugins
- `scout:install` — Integration with sandbox isolation
- Security skill implementing 6-point advisory scan
- Domain-specific skills for evaluation criteria

### Humanize Content (Superpower [11], Mar 14, 2026)
Making AI-generated content sound human:

- Core humanization pipeline skill
- Banned vocabulary reference (overused AI phrases)
- LinkedIn-specific rules and constraints
- Wired into the content creation commands

### Social Media Posting (Superpower [10], Mar 13-14, 2026)

- Content database schema extended for social media types

Discovered **18 months of social media post templates** stored in Notion — a goldmine of proven content patterns:

- Imported 29+ templates covering March-August 2024 period

  - Anaphora patterns ("Embrace discomfort...")
  - Reversal frameworks ("Old Era vs New Era")

- Template engine designed with JSON-based format for structure and metadata

**Build-in-Public Angle:** *"18 months of LinkedIn posts, analyzed and turned into reusable templates. Every post I've ever written is now a template my AI can remix."*

**Act 1: The Spark** (Jan 30 - Feb 11)
*From LinkedIn automation agents to the vision of a complete founder operating system*

**Act 2: The Factory** (Feb 11 - Mar 7)
*30 plugins built using a repeatable system, Notion HQ consolidated, UAT testing proves quality*

**Act 3: The Pivot** (Mar 11)
*Single plugin restructure, marketplace distribution, intelligence engine — the architecture matures*

**Act 4: The Superpowers** (Mar 12 - Mar 16)
*16 numbered design specs, platform-agnostic content creation, NPM distribution, self-learning capabilities*

**Act 5: Going Public** (Now)
*The build-in-public journey begins — sharing the timeline, the lessons, and the tool itself*

---

*Last updated: March 16, 2026*
*Source: 6,300+ development observations across 600+ AI-assisted sessions*
*Total development investment: ~250,000+ tokens of research, building, and decision-making*

Done. The file is at `docs/founder-os-development-timeline.md`.

Here's what's in it:

**7 major sections** covering the full 46-day arc from Jan 30 to Mar 16, 2026:

1. **Origin Story** — LinkedIn outreach agents that sparked the vision
2. **30-Plugin Vision** — Blueprint day, first plugin (with the dramatic reset), plugin factory cadence
3. **Platform Emergence** — OpenViber web app, NaluForge landing page, UAT testing (96.4% pass rate)
4. **Architecture Revolution** — Single plugin restructure, marketplace pivot, intelligence engine
5. **Superpowers Era** — 16 numbered design specs, evals framework, scout namespace, humanize content
6. **Content Creation System** — 29+ social templates imported, ideate namespace refactor
7. **Distribution & Packaging** — NPM distribution, `npx founder-os --init`

Plus **ready-to-use content seeds**:
- 7 blog post ideas with angles
- 10 LinkedIn post hooks
- 7 X/Twitter posts
- 10 Reel/TikTok concepts
- 8 technical story angles for developer audiences
- A 5-act narrative arc summary

All sourced from 6,300+ observations across 600+ AI sessions. Take your time going through it — there's a lot of content to mine.

    "title": "LinkedIn Contact Finder CLAUDE.md Defines Dual-Mode Prospecting System",
    "subtitle": "Operator guide for discovering LinkedIn profiles via browser extension or Apify batch processing with Notion synchronization.",
    "facts": "[\"LinkedIn Contact Finder operates in two modes: browser extension for real-time interactive discovery and Apify batch for large-scale automated prospecting\",\"LinkedInContact model uses linkedin_url as unique identifier with 16 fields including name, company, job_title, industry, seniority, activity_level, and batch_id\",\"Mandatory deduplication workflow checks linkedin_url existence before saving, updating existing records instead of creating duplicates\",\"NotionSyncManager handles contact synchronization with merge logic for existing records and new record creation\",\"BatchOrchestrator executes Apify batch searches with BatchConfig supporting dry_run mode, max_results limits, and cost controls\",\"Rate limiting enforced at Notion 3 req/sec, Apify actor-specific limits, and browser mode 2-5 second human-like delays\",\"WAT Framework structure with workflows (markdown SOPs for browser and batch search), tools (Python scripts), and models (Pydantic dataclasses)\",\"Security protocol requires API keys in .env file with hashed URL identifiers in logs and no credential storage\"]",
    "narrative": "The linkedin_contact_finder CLAUDE.md establishes an operator guide for a dual-mode LinkedIn prospecting system that discovers and manages contacts through either real-time browser interaction or scheduled bulk processing. The system's core architecture supports two distinct workflows: browser extension mode for interactive small-batch discovery using MCP claude-in-chrome integration, and Apify batch mode for large-scale automation with cost controls and dry-run capabilities. The LinkedInContact data model captures comprehensive profile information with linkedin_url as the unique identifier, enabling deduplication logic that prevents duplicate records by checking existing database entries and merging data into existing records when matches are found. NotionSyncManager orchestrates the synchronization workflow, while BatchOrchestrator handles Apify actor execution with configurable search strategies, result limits, and cost thresholds. Rate limiting adapts to the operating mode, enforcing strict Notion API limits (3 req/sec) and implementing human-like delays (2-5 seconds) in browser mode to avoid detection. The WAT Framework pattern separates concerns between workflows (SOPs for browser-search.md and batch-search.md), tools (Python scripts for Notion, Apify, deduplication), and models (enums for Industry, CompanySize, Seniority, ActivityLevel).",

    "subtitle": "New agent planned to generate personalized connection request messages with adjustable tone and purpose.",
    "facts": "[\"Agent will copy contacts from LinkedIn Contact table to new table with Ice Breaker column\",\"Ice breaker messages will be generated for LinkedIn connection requests with customizable purpose and tone\",\"Use cases include casual networking (compelling, open-ended, nice) and specific requests (asking for advice from top managers about pain points)\",\"Purpose and tone parameters will be configurable per contact as input parameters\"]",
    "narrative": "A new agent feature is being designed to automate personalized LinkedIn outreach.   {

    "memory_session_id": "96ec73b4-acc0-4955-a76b-38adde9f4910",

    "text": null,
    "type": "decision",
    "title": "EnrichmentManager Orchestrator Design Established",
    "subtitle": "Created architecture for main enrichment orchestrator class managing profile data collection and Notion updates.",
    "facts": "[\"EnrichmentManager class will be created in tools/enrichment_manager.py\",\"Primary method enrich_contacts() processes List[Contact] and returns EnrichmentResult\",\"Enrichment workflow: navigate to profile, parse data, update Notion for each contact\",\"Browser rate limiter enforces delays between profile visits to avoid LinkedIn detection\",\"Progress and error tracking built into orchestration logic\",\"Single contact enrichment via enrich_single() method returns success/error dictionary\",\"Enrichment process uses MCP for page text retrieval with user-initiated navigation\",\"LinkedInProfileParser handles data extraction from profile pages\",\"Notion updates performed via existing notion_client infrastructure\",\"EnrichmentResult dataclass tracks enriched count, skipped count, errors count, and error_details list\",\"Dependencies include models, parser, notion_client, and rate_limiter modules\"]",
    "narrative": "The enrichment manager architecture defines the main orchestrator class that coordinates the lead enrichment workflow. EnrichmentManager follows the pattern established by NotionSyncManager and BatchOrchestrator in the existing codebase, providing a high-level interface for enrichment operations. The core method enrich_contacts() processes batches of contacts, while enrich_single() handles individual contact enrichment. The workflow involves rate-limited navigation to LinkedIn profiles, MCP-based page text extraction (with user navigation followed by agent reading), LinkedInProfileParser for data extraction, and Notion updates via the existing client infrastructure. The browser_limiter component enforces delays between profile visits to maintain human-like browsing patterns and avoid LinkedIn rate limiting or detection. EnrichmentResult provides structured reporting of operation outcomes similar to SyncResult in the sync manager. This design positions EnrichmentManager as the primary tool that workflow markdown files will reference for enrichment operations.",

  {
    "id": 61,

    "text": null,
    "type": "decision",
    "title": "Lead Enrichment Data Model Architecture Defined",
    "subtitle": "Decided to extend Contact model with enrichment fields and create EnrichmentStatus enum for tracking.",
    "facts": "[\"Lead enrichment agent will extend Contact model in models/contact.py with new enrichment-specific fields\",\"New fields include profile_summary for About section content\",\"Recent post links stored as List[str] with maximum 5 URLs\",\"Post topics stored as comma-separated string for theme analysis\",\"Contact info field stores public email, phone, and website data\",\"Company website URL field added for company details\",\"Shared connections count tracked as integer field\",\"Last enriched timestamp using datetime for tracking enrichment freshness\",\"EnrichmentStatus enum created with states: not_enriched, in_progress, enriched, failed\",\"Models follow existing patterns from linkedin_contact_finder/models/contact.py including to_notion_properties() and from_notion_page() serialization methods\"]",
    "narrative": "The architecture design phase established the data model strategy for the lead enrichment agent. Rather than creating a separate enrichment data model, the decision was made to extend the existing Contact model with enrichment-specific fields. This approach maintains data cohesion and simplifies the Notion integration since all contact data resides in a single model and database record. The enrichment fields cover the user's requirements: profile_summary captures the About section, recent_post_links stores up to 5 post URLs as a list, post_topics holds comma-separated theme keywords, contact_info aggregates public contact details, company_website stores the company URL, shared_connections tracks network overlap as an integer, and last_enriched timestamps the enrichment operation. An EnrichmentStatus enum will track the enrichment lifecycle with four states. The implementation will follow the existing linkedin_contact_finder patterns for Notion serialization through to_notion_properties() and from_notion_page() methods, ensuring compatibility with the existing NotionSyncManager infrastructure.",

    "type": "decision",
    "title": "Lead Enrichment Agent Structured as Independent Codebase",
    "subtitle": "Created separate lead_enrichment_agent directory following WAT framework rather than extending linkedin_contact_finder codebase.",
    "facts": "[\"Lead enrichment agent structured as independent project in lead_enrichment_agent/ directory\",\"Follows WAT Framework structure: CLAUDE.md for agent instructions, models/ for data structures, tools/ for Python scripts, workflows/ for markdown SOPs, config/ for settings\",\"Project setup includes .env.example for credentials template, .gitignore for source control, requirements.txt for dependencies\",\"Decision to create separate agent codebase rather than extending linkedin_contact_finder for clean separation of concerns\",\"Pattern reuse from linkedin_contact_finder but maintaining codebase independence\"]",
    "narrative": "The lead enrichment agent was architected as an independent codebase in a new lead_enrichment_agent/ directory rather than extending the existing linkedin_contact_finder project. This architectural decision maintains clean separation between contact discovery and enrichment operations while allowing pattern reuse. The new agent follows the same WAT Framework structure (Workflows, Agent, Tools) with CLAUDE.md for agent instructions, models/ for Pydantic data structures, tools/ for Python utilities, workflows/ for markdown SOPs, and config/ for environment-based settings. Standard project files include .env.example for credential templates, .gitignore for version control exclusions, and requirements.txt for dependency management. This independent structure enables each agent to evolve separately, prevents tight coupling between discovery and enrichment logic, and maintains the single responsibility principle at the project level.",

    "title": "Separate Lead Enrichment Agent Architecture with Backlog-Based Parallel Development",

    "facts": "[\"Lead enrichment agent will be separate new agent, not extension of existing linkedin_contact_finder\",\"Architecture design phase should not proceed to implementation but create structured backlog instead\",\"Backlog structure optimized for parallel development by multiple agents working independently\",\"Decision enables independent evolution of contact discovery and lead enrichment capabilities\",\"Separation allows different agents to work on contact finder and enrichment agent simultaneously\"]",
    "narrative": "The user made a critical architectural decision to create a completely separate lead enrichment agent rather than extending the existing linkedin_contact_finder system. This separation strategy enables parallel development where multiple AI agents can work independently on different components without conflicts. The user specifically requested that once the architecture is designed, the work should not move to implementation but instead create a structured backlog using \"beads\" (likely referring to a task breakdown format). This backlog-driven approach optimizes for concurrent development by clearly delineating independent work streams that can be executed in parallel. The decision reflects a modular architecture philosophy where the contact discovery system and lead enrichment system remain loosely coupled, allowing each to evolve independently while sharing data through the Notion database.",

For the Client Context Loader, this would likely show examples like \"/client:load Acme Corp\" with an explanation that it creates a complete dossier in under 30 seconds, and tips like \"run this before client calls to refresh your memory\" or \"use /client:brief for quick prep when time is tight.\" The troubleshooting section anticipates the most common failure modes (MCP configuration issues, permissions) without overwhelming users with edge cases.     "facts": "[\"Plugin uses a pipeline pattern with 4 sequential agents: Triage, Action, Response, Archive\",\"Two operational modes: default single-agent summary and --team flag for full pipeline execution\",\"MCP server integrations include Gmail (required), Notion (optional), and Calendar (optional)\",\"Two user-facing commands will be implemented: /inbox:triage and /inbox:drafts_approved\",\"Architecture follows Clean Architecture principles with 5 decomposed skills\",\"Plugin is designated as the first Founder OS reference implementation\",\"Plugin identifier is #01 in the Founder OS ecosystem\"]",
    "narrative": "The Inbox Zero Commander plugin is architected as a multi-agent pipeline system for email management. The core design features four specialized agents (Triage, Action, Response, Archive) that process emails sequentially. Users can operate in two modes: a lightweight single-agent summary for quick triage, or a comprehensive --team mode that runs the full agent pipeline. External integrations leverage MCP servers for Gmail (mandatory for email access), Notion (optional for task/project management), and Calendar (optional for scheduling). The implementation follows Clean Architecture principles by decomposing functionality into 5 discrete skills that can be tested and maintained independently. Two commands provide user interaction points for initiating triage workflows and managing approved draft responses. This serves as the foundational reference implementation for the Founder OS plugin ecosystem.",

    "subtitle": "Consolidated 33 plugin directories into single plugin and updated tooling for marketplace distribution model",
    "facts": "[\"Development repository pushed to https://github.com/thecloudtips/founderOS.git\",\"Removed 33 old plugin directories, completing migration to single plugin structure (commit fe3110a)\",\"Release script updated for marketplace plugin structure (commit 144c486)\",\"Installer specification documentation updated for marketplace distribution model (commit 777929f)\"]",
    "narrative": "The founderOS development repository underwent major refactoring to support a marketplace distribution model. The project was consolidated from 33 separate plugin directories into a single unified plugin structure. Supporting this change, the release script was updated to handle the new marketplace plugin structure, and the installer specification documentation was revised to reflect the marketplace distribution model.
